generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor capturing raw point cloud data for environmental representation and object tracking in autonomous systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle telemetry sensor providing real-time state data including speed, position, and orientation for digital twin synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted via Wi-Fi/UDP network for real-time processing"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle telemetry data transmitted via Wi-Fi/UDP network for digital twin synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AI-driven clustering algorithm converting raw LiDAR points into structured object tracks and environmental maps"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time kinematic model processing vehicle telemetry for position, velocity, and orientation estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and environmental representations derived from raw LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data including filtered telemetry and coordinate transformations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and driving paths derived from LiDAR data for adaptive cruise control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PathPlanningEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component implementing Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking algorithm for LiDAR data processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance VehicleStateProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component processing vehicle telemetry data for state estimation and coordinate transformations"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateMonitoringService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component ensuring data serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversions across components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance PathPlanningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component generating and optimizing driving paths based on object tracks and vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:enables PathPlanningService
]

instance ControlSystemEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system processing actuation feedback for autonomous vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleControlService
]

instance EdgeComputingPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-aligned edge computing platform providing low-latency data processing for real-time applications"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeProcessingService
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulator for thermal/structural modeling of battery systems and vehicle components"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance AiDrivenSimulationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI/ML-driven digital twin simulation framework for prognostic algorithms and predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:enables FailurePredictionService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering tool supporting ISO 23247-compliant digital twin development"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinDevelopmentService
]

instance ServiceOrientedArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service-Oriented Architecture enabling modular digital twin components with microservices"
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for storing IoT sensor data with high temporal resolution"
]

instance SemanticDataLake : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ontology-based semantic data lake enabling interoperability through knowledge graphs"
]

instance DigitalThreadFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "End-to-end digital thread framework linking design, manufacturing, and operational phases"
]

instance CyberPhysicalSystemIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization layer between physical assets and digital replicas"
]

instance AdaptationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Domain-specific adaptation layer for specialized applications like aerospace collision avoidance"
]

// C6: Services
instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time object detection and tracking from LiDAR data for autonomous navigation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackingInsight
]

instance VehicleStateMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service monitoring vehicle state parameters including speed, position, and orientation"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight
]

instance PathPlanningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating optimal driving paths based on object tracks and vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:provides PathPlanningInsight
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing adaptive cruise control using processed object tracks and driving paths"
    DTDFVocab:provides CruiseControlAction
]

instance VehicleControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing closed-loop control of vehicle actuators based on system feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleControlAction
]

instance RealTimeProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling low-latency data processing for real-time applications on edge platforms"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeProcessingInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using physics-based and AI-driven simulations for predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenancePredictionInsight
]

instance FailurePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service predicting component failures using digital twin-based prognostic algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring seamless data integration across heterogeneous components"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataIntegrationInsight
]

instance DigitalTwinDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting model-driven development of ISO 23247-compliant digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:provides DevelopmentInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D/4D digital replicas with interactive dashboards for monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

    base:desc "Real-time environmental awareness through structured object tracks and spatial mapping"
]

instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current vehicle state including position, velocity, and orientation for navigation systems"
]

instance PathPlanningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimal driving path recommendations based on environmental conditions and vehicle capabilities"
]

instance CruiseControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated speed and distance regulation based on object tracking data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VehicleControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated actuation commands for steering and propulsion systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RealTimeProcessingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Low-latency processing capabilities for real-time decision making"
]

instance MaintenancePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted maintenance requirements based on component degradation models"
]

]

    base:desc "Seamless integration of heterogeneous data sources across the digital twin ecosystem"
]

]

    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D representations of physical systems for monitoring and analysis"
]

// C1: Physical Twin Systems (Environments)
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Automotive system environment including vehicle components, sensors, and actuators"
    base:contains LiDARSensor, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ManufacturingSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial IoT-enabled manufacturing environment with cyber-physical production systems"
    base:contains DigitalThreadFramework, CyberPhysicalSystemIntegration
]

instance AerospaceSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Space-based asset environment with collision avoidance and debris tracking capabilities"
    base:contains AdaptationLayer
]

instance BatterySystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Energy management system environment with thermal and structural modeling"
    base:contains PhysicsBasedSimulationEngine
]

// C1: Digital Twin Entities (Components)
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Central digital twin entity containing all software components for processing and control"
    base:contains PointCloudProcessingEnabler, VehicleStateProcessingEnabler,
                   DataTranslationEnabler, PathPlanningEnabler, ControlSystemEnabler,
                   EdgeComputingPlatform, PhysicsBasedSimulationEngine, AiDrivenSimulationFramework,
                   ModelDrivenEngineeringTool, ServiceOrientedArchitecture, TimeSeriesDatabase,
                   SemanticDataLake, DigitalThreadFramework, CyberPhysicalSystemIntegration,
                   AdaptationLayer
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical twin entity containing inherent system resources and automotive functionalities"
    base:contains LiDARSensor, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface entity providing monitoring and control interfaces"
    base:contains VisualizationService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves control flows from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity**, governed by the following technical specifications:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Motor Actuation Commands**: Generated by the **Controlling FE** (e.g., for ACC or autonomous driving) and sent to the **Motor Actuation FE** to adjust throttle or braking.
   - **Steering Servo Commands**: Sent from the **Controlling FE** to the **Steering Servo Actuation FE** to modify steering angle.
   - **Vehicle State Overrides**: Potential commands to adjust vehicle parameters (e.g., dynamic stability control) via the **Vehicle State FE**.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Standardized via the **Data Translation FE**, which ensures **serialization** and **message type alignment** for control signals (e.g., ROS2 message formats for motor/servo commands).
   - Likely includes **structured payloads** with fields for command type, target values (e.g., steering angle in degrees), and execution parameters.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but implied by the **Data Translation FE**’s role in ensuring message integrity and consistency.
   - Potential reliance on **ROS2’s built-in topic-based communication** for basic validation (e.g., message schema compliance).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Actuation Feedback Loop**: The **Motor** and **Steering Servo Actuation FE** provide real-time feedback (e.g., actual motor torque, steering angle) back to the **Controlling FE** for closed-loop control.
   - **Vehicle State Updates**: The **Vehicle State FE** continuously reports physical state changes (e.g., speed, position) to the Digital Twin for validation against commanded actions.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Not explicitly specified**, but the **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC logic) likely implements priority-based arbitration for conflicting commands (e.g., emergency braking vs. steering adjustments).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - Implied via **feedback loops** from the **Actuation FE** to the **Controlling FE**, ensuring commands are executed as intended (e.g., confirmed steering angle matches the commanded value).
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination. This temporal granularity is mandated by hardware limitations and aligns with safety-critical vehicular applications, though stricter synchronization (e.g., <100 ms) is required by some standards. The system leverages **clock synchronization mechanisms** to ensure temporal alignment across distributed components, particularly for real-time services like sensor data processing, actuation, and device communication. Latency requirements are implicitly constrained by the 100 ms threshold, which governs the maximum acceptable delay for time-sensitive operations within the Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity. No explicit mention of multi-tiered time scales (e.g., microsecond-level for control vs. millisecond-level for visualization) is provided, but the 100 ms constraint applies uniformly to all time-critical services."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its constellation through a **ROS2-based microservices architecture**, where each functional entity (*Physical Twin*, *Digital Twin*, *User*) is implemented as an **independent ROS2 package** containing one or more ROS2 nodes. This design aligns with the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)** but introduces deviations, such as the *Physical Twin Entity* hosting general automotive functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) beyond resource-specific operations. Component integration is achieved via **ROS2 inter-node communication**, enabling real-time data exchange between sensors/actuators (Physical Twin), data management/control mechanisms (Digital Twin), and user-facing interfaces (User Entity). Resource management is implicit in the entity-based structure, with hardware-specific deployments dictating ownership of functionalities. No explicit system-wide coordination protocol (e.g., consensus algorithms, event-driven buses) is mentioned; instead, the architecture relies on **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model** and the ISO 23247 RA’s entity relationships for orchestration. The absence of certain ISO 23247 Function Elements (FEs) and the splitting of the *User Interface FE* into subcomponents further tailor the constellation to the system’s requirements."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **ISO 23247 standard** does not explicitly detail **fidelity requirements** (e.g., resolution, granularity) for Digital Twin models or data. However, the **entity-based architecture** implies that validation and verification (V&V) must align with the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**'s operational accuracy. While the standard does not prescribe specific **uncertainty quantification methods** or **error correction mechanisms**, it establishes a framework where **Data Collection and Device Control Entities** must ensure data integrity for real-time monitoring and control. Quality assurance processes are indirectly addressed through the **User Entity**, which provides interfaces for human oversight, suggesting a reliance on **user-driven validation** of model outputs. The standard does not mention **sampling frequencies, precision specifications, or formal V&V methodologies** (e.g., model calibration, statistical testing), leaving these as implementation-dependent considerations."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is defined by the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**: **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** network for publish-subscribe communication between software components (e.g., **Presentation FE**, **Controlling FE**, **Data Translation FE**).
   - **Inter-Entity (Physical-Digital)**: **Wi-Fi network using UDP** in the transport layer for low-latency data exchange between the **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/vehicle) and **Digital Twin Entity** (software).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical Model**:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: Sources raw data (LiDAR, motor/servo states, vehicle telemetry).
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosts software components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**, **Presentation FE**) communicating via ROS2.
     - **User Entity**: Interfaces with the Digital Twin via **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** (Fig. 3).
   - **Data Flow Path**: Sensor data → **Data Pre-Processing FE** → **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking** → **Controlling FE** → **Presentation FE** → **User Interface**.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Not explicitly quantified**, but implied to be **low-latency** for real-time applications (e.g., LiDAR point cloud processing, ACC control).
   - **UDP Transport**: Prioritizes speed over reliability for time-critical data (e.g., vehicle state updates).

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Data Translation FE**: Ensures **message consistency** and **unit conversion** across components, reducing protocol mismatches.
   - **ROS2 Topics**: May include **redundant publishers** or **retry mechanisms** for critical data (e.g., vehicle state).
   - **Wi-Fi Redundancy**: Not specified, but UDP’s stateless nature may require application-layer recovery for dropped packets.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**. Potential reliance on:
     - **ROS2 Security Extensions** (e.g., TLS for encrypted communication between components).
     - **Wi-Fi Encryption** (e.g., WPA3) for inter-entity communication.
     - **Authentication**: Implied via **Data Translation FE**’s role in validating message formats, though no specific mechanisms are described.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Implied by the **on-board processing** of sensor data (e.g., LiDAR clustering, motor/servo control) to minimize latency.
   - **On-Premise (Vehicle-Integrated)**: The **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors, actuators) and **Digital Twin Entity** (software components) are co-located within the vehicle, suggesting a **vehicle-centric deployment model**.
   - **Cloud Not Explicitly Mentioned**: No evidence of cloud hosting; all processing appears localized to the vehicle’s embedded systems.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Computational**:
     - **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**: Requires significant processing power for real-time LiDAR data analysis (e.g., GPU acceleration for point cloud operations).
     - **Controlling FE**: Demands low-latency computation for ACC logic and actuator control.
     - **Data Translation FE**: Lightweight but must handle high-throughput data serialization.
   - **Storage**:
     - **Data Storage FE**: Likely stores processed data (e.g., object tracks, reports) for short-term analysis or logging.
     - **No Cloud Storage Specified**: Storage appears localized, with potential for **on-vehicle flash memory** or **SSD** for high-speed access.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Modular ROS2 Architecture**: Enables horizontal scaling by adding redundant nodes or components (e.g., multiple **Controlling FE** instances for different driving modes).
   - **Component-Based Design**: Software services (e.g., **Presentation FE**, **Reporting FE**) can be scaled independently based on demand.
   - **Not Explicitly Distributed**: No mention of distributed computing (e.g., Kubernetes) or cloud scaling; scalability is limited to vehicle-integrated resources.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Single-Vehicle Deployment**: Primarily designed for **individual vehicle use cases** (e.g., autonomous driving, ADAS).
   - **No Multi-Vehicle or Fleet Scaling**: No evidence of support for distributed Digital Twins across multiple vehicles or centralized orchestration.

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Redundancy**: Implied via **feedback loops** (e.g., actuation confirmation) and **ROS2’s fault-tolerant design** (e.g., node restarts).
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for **safety-critical applications** (e.g., ACC), but no explicit redundancy (e.g., backup sensors or processors) is described.
   - **Data Persistence**: **Reporting FE** suggests periodic data logging, but no high-availability storage mechanisms (e.g., RAID, replication) are mentioned.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)**, which defines an **entity-based framework** for manufacturing DTs. Key compliance elements include:
  - **Entity-based architecture**: Mandates four core entities (**Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)**, and **User Entity**), each with defined responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability**: While not explicitly prescribed, the standard implies compatibility with protocols like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange between entities (e.g., DTE ↔ Data Collection Entity).
  - **Certification**: No explicit certification requirements are mentioned, but adherence to ISO 23247 ensures alignment with **manufacturing-specific DT frameworks**.
  - **Compliance frameworks**: The standard provides a **modular, entity-centric** approach, ensuring traceability and consistency across DT implementations. **Information exchange** (ISO 23247 Part 4) is referenced but not detailed in the provided context."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system, particularly in the context of **ISO 26262-1:2018 (Road vehicles – Functional safety)**, adheres to **functional safety standards** for safety-critical systems, including **automotive applications** (e.g., adaptive cruise control systems as referenced in **ISO 15622:2018**). This involves:
  - **Risk assessment methodologies** aligned with **ISO 26262**, which defines **Automotive Safety Integrity Levels (ASIL)** for system components, ensuring compliance with **safety requirements specifications** and **hazard analysis** (e.g., FMEA, FTA).
  - **Fail-safe mechanisms** and **redundancy protocols** to mitigate system failures, particularly in **safety-critical subsystems** (e.g., braking, steering, or communication modules).
  - **Cybersecurity protocols** implicitly tied to **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as outlined in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, which mandates **secure communication channels** (e.g., **authentication, encryption, and integrity protection**) for **inter-vehicle and infrastructure communication** to prevent unauthorized access or data tampering.
  - **Access control mechanisms** for **model-driven architectures** (as discussed in **Dalibor et al., 2020**), where **role-based permissions** and **digital twin cockpit interfaces** enforce **least-privilege access** to sensitive system states or control parameters.
  - **Threat mitigation strategies** for **real-time system interactions**, including **intrusion detection systems (IDS)** and **secure boot processes** to ensure **tamper-proof execution** of safety-critical functions.
  - **Compliance with automotive-grade security standards** (e.g., **ISO/SAE 21434** for cybersecurity engineering, though not explicitly cited, is implied by the context of **ISO 26262** and **3GPP V2X requirements**)."
]
